title: DL-022-evaluation-metrics
author: AI 面试题库
abstract:
DL-022 ⭐⭐⭐ Model Evaluation Metrics for Classification and Regression

 Question
What are common evaluation metrics in deep learning? Explain metrics for classification and regression tasks.

 Answer

 Why Multiple Metrics?

Accuracy alone can be misleading:
- Imbalanced datasets: 99% accuracy with 99% negative samples
- Different costs: False positive vs false negative
- Need comprehensive evaluation


---

 1. Accuracy

sections.title: Question
sections.content: What are common evaluation metrics in deep learning? Explain metrics for classification and regression tasks.

sections.title: Why Multiple Metrics?
sections.content:
```
Accuracy alone can be misleading:
- Imbalanced datasets: 99% accuracy with 99% negative samples
- Different costs: False positive vs false negative
- Need comprehensive evaluation
```

---

sections.title: 1. Accuracy
sections.content:
```
Accuracy = (TP + TN) / (TP + TN + FP + FN)

✅ Simple, intuitive
❌ Misleading for imbalanced datasets
```

sections.title: 2. Precision
sections.content:
```
Precision = TP / (TP + FP)

"How many predicted positives are actually positive?"

Use when: False positives are costly (spam detection)
```

sections.title: 3. Recall (Sensitivity)
sections.content:
```
Recall = TP / (TP + FN)

"How many actual positives did we find?"

Use when: False negatives are costly (disease detection)
```

sections.title: 4. F1-Score
sections.content:
```
F1 = 2 × (Precision × Recall) / (Precision + Recall)

Harmonic mean of precision and recall
Good balance when both matter
```

sections.title: 5. ROC-AUC
sections.content:
```
ROC Curve: TPR vs FPR at different thresholds
AUC: Area under ROC curve (0.5 = random, 1.0 = perfect)

✅ Threshold-independent
✅ Good for imbalanced datasets
```

sections.title: 6. Precision-Recall AUC
sections.content:
```
Better than ROC-AUC for highly imbalanced datasets
Focuses on positive class performance
```

---

sections.title: 1. Mean Absolute Error (MAE)
sections.content:
```
MAE = (1/n) × Σ|y_pred - y_true|

✅ Interpretable (average error)
✅ Robust to outliers
```

sections.title: 2. Mean Squared Error (MSE)
sections.content:
```
MSE = (1/n) × Σ(y_pred - y_true)²

✅ Penalizes large errors
❌ Not in same units as target
```

sections.title: 3. Root Mean Squared Error (RMSE)
sections.content:
```
RMSE = √MSE

✅ Same units as target
✅ Penalizes large errors
```

sections.title: 4. R² Score (Coefficient of Determination)
sections.content:
```
R² = 1 - (SS_res / SS_tot)

✅ Interpretable (variance explained)
✅ Range: -∞ to 1 (1 = perfect)
```

---

sections.title: Key Takeaways
sections.content:
- Classification: Accuracy, Precision, Recall, F1, ROC-AUC
- Regression: MAE, MSE, RMSE, R²
- Choose metrics based on business objectives
- Use multiple metrics for comprehensive evaluation
